steps.name: Bridge core types in overdrive-core (State, View, Action variant, AnyReconciler/AnyState arms)
steps.description: Land the bridge's pure type surface in `overdrive-core` per architecture.md § 4.2. (1) Add `BackendDiscoveryBridgeState { desired: ServiceListenerSet, actual: RunningAllocSet }` (per ADR-0036 — runtime stitches desired + actual into one struct before reconcile). `ServiceListenerSet { workload_id, listeners: BTreeMap<ServiceId, ProjectedListener> }`. `ProjectedListener { vip: ServiceVip, port: NonZeroU16, protocol: Proto }` — the VIP is allocator-issued (sourced at hydrate time per ADR-0049 § 5a), NOT carried by intent. `RunningAllocSet { workload_id, running: BTreeSet<AllocationId> }`. (2) Add `BackendDiscoveryBridgeView { last_written_fingerprint: BTreeMap<ServiceId, BackendSetFingerprint> }` with `#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Default, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]` and `#[serde(default)]` on the field — per ADR-0035 the runtime owns CBOR persistence end-to-end; bridge author derives the four mandatory bounds only. The persisted input is the per-service fingerprint of the last row successfully written (per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Persist inputs, not derived state"). (3) Add new `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow { row: ServiceBackendRow, correlation: CorrelationKey }` variant to the Action enum per architecture.md § 4.3. (4) Add `BackendDiscoveryBridge` variant to `AnyReconciler` and `BackendDiscoveryBridge(BackendDiscoveryBridgeState)` variant to `AnyState`. (5) MUST use `BTreeMap` / `BTreeSet` throughout (NOT `HashMap` / `HashSet`) per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Ordered-collection choice" — fingerprint maps are iterated by the bridge's reconcile loop and DST invariants assert on iteration order. (6) `BackendSetFingerprint` is a `u64` newtype or transparent alias derived from a deterministic hash of `(vip_bytes, sorted_backend_entries)`; place under `overdrive-core::reconciler::backend_discovery_bridge` module. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md`: every match site over `AnyReconciler` / `AnyState` becomes non-exhaustive and the compile-time match-exhaustiveness check (per architecture.md § 8.4 / ADR-0023) forces every consumer into the new arm in this commit. No reconcile body yet (that's 01-02); no hydration arms yet (that's 01-03); no action-shim dispatch yet (that's 01-04). The struct surface is the deliberate seam.
steps.criteria: Workspace `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean. `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` clean. New `BackendDiscoveryBridgeState`, `ServiceListenerSet`, `ProjectedListener`, `RunningAllocSet`, `BackendDiscoveryBridgeView`, `BackendSetFingerprint` types exist in `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconciler/backend_discovery_bridge.rs` (or equivalent module path) with rustdoc on every public item per `.claude/rules/development.md` § Documentation. `BackendDiscoveryBridgeView` derives `Serialize + Deserialize + Default + Clone` (the four mandatory bounds per ADR-0035) and `PartialEq + Eq` (for DST equality assertions). `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow { row, correlation }` variant exists in the Action enum; every match site over Action is updated (exhaustive); the `match action { ... }` enforcement compiles. `AnyReconciler::BackendDiscoveryBridge(BackendDiscoveryBridge)` variant exists; `AnyState::BackendDiscoveryBridge(BackendDiscoveryBridgeState)` variant exists; every match over `AnyReconciler` / `AnyState` is updated. NO `HashMap` / `HashSet` in the new types (grep returns zero matches; `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Ordered-collection choice" — fingerprint maps are iterated by the DST invariants and by the bridge's reconcile body). RED scaffold gates remain: invariant stubs in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/backend_discovery_bridge.rs` still `todo!()` (closed in 01-05); walking_skeleton.rs + boot_composition.rs RED `#[should_panic]` tests untouched. CBOR round-trip property test for `BackendDiscoveryBridgeView` (ciborium serialize → deserialize → equal-to-original) lands in this step in a unit-test sibling file (NOT acceptance) since this exercises only the pure type surface.
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: (no direct acceptance scenario — pre-stages 01-02) | Unit tests: backend_discovery_bridge_view_cbor_roundtrip, backend_discovery_bridge_view_serde_default_tolerates_unknown_fields, action_write_service_backend_row_variant_constructs, any_reconciler_backend_discovery_bridge_variant_constructs
steps.implementation_notes: ProjectedListener.vip is `ServiceVip` (allocator-issued at hydrate time per ADR-0049 § 5a / ADR-0050 § 2 — Listener has NO vip field at intent layer). The fingerprint type is a `u64` derived from a deterministic hash; use a small pure helper `fingerprint(&ServiceVip, &[Backend]) -> BackendSetFingerprint` whose body is in 01-02 (this step lands the type signature only, behind a stubbed body that lands in 01-02). Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Hashing requires deterministic serialization": internal fingerprint can use rkyv archived bytes or a hand-rolled little-endian byte concatenation — choose whichever yields the smallest single-purpose helper. NOTE: an `impl Reconciler for BackendDiscoveryBridge` block is NOT landed in this step — the reconcile body lands in 01-02, and the `BackendDiscoveryBridge` struct itself lands in 01-02 alongside its impl. This step lands only the State/View/Action surface; the AnyReconciler variant is a placeholder enum carrying a unit type until 01-02 widens it. Use `AnyReconciler::BackendDiscoveryBridge(())` placeholder OR introduce a 1-line empty `BackendDiscoveryBridge` struct here to satisfy the variant type — crafter picks at PREPARE per the smallest single-cut diff.

steps.name: BackendDiscoveryBridge struct + reconcile body + fingerprint pure decision fn
steps.description: Land the bridge struct + its `Reconciler` trait impl + the pure `fingerprint` decision fn at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/backend_discovery_bridge/mod.rs` per architecture.md § 4.1-4.2. The reconcile body is pure-sync per ADR-0035 (no `.await`, no I/O, no DB handle, no wall-clock except `tick.now`); fully DST-replayable. (1) `BackendDiscoveryBridge { name: ReconcilerName, host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr, writer_node_id: NodeId }`. (2) `impl Reconciler for BackendDiscoveryBridge` with `type State = BackendDiscoveryBridgeState; type View = BackendDiscoveryBridgeView;` and `fn reconcile(&self, desired, actual, view, tick) -> (Vec<Action>, Self::View)` exactly as architecture.md § 4.2 specifies — loop over `desired.desired.listeners.iter()`, build `backends: Vec<Backend>` from `actual.actual.running` (one Backend per Running alloc; Phase 2.2 single-node so every alloc resolves to `self.host_ipv4`), compute `new_fp = fingerprint(&listener.vip, &backends)`, dedup against `view.last_written_fingerprint.get(service_id)`, emit `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow` if different, GC `next_view.last_written_fingerprint.retain(|sid, _| desired.desired.listeners.contains_key(sid))`. (3) `LogicalTimestamp { counter: tick.tick.saturating_add(1), writer: self.writer_node_id.clone() }` on the emitted row's `updated_at`. (4) `Backend` is the existing type at `overdrive-core::Backend { ipv4, port, weight, healthy, _pad }` per ServiceBackendRow shape. (5) The `fingerprint` pure fn lives in the same module as the reconciler (not in core — it's an internal implementation detail of the bridge); takes `(&ServiceVip, &[Backend]) -> BackendSetFingerprint`; SHA-256 or rkyv-archive over canonical bytes per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Hashing requires deterministic serialization". (6) `mod.rs` re-exports + `ReconcilerName` constant `"backend-discovery-bridge"`. (7) NO hydration arm code in this step (lands 01-03); NO action-shim dispatch (lands 01-04); the reconciler is registered in production boot in 01-04. NO RED scaffolds closed in this step — DST invariants close in 01-05 once the full surface lands. NOTE: per architecture.md § 4.7, the bridge does NOT hold an `Arc<Mutex<PersistentServiceVipAllocator>>` — the allocator lookup is at the runtime hydrate boundary, NOT in `reconcile`.
steps.criteria: `BackendDiscoveryBridge` struct exists at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/backend_discovery_bridge/mod.rs`. `impl Reconciler for BackendDiscoveryBridge` body matches architecture.md § 4.2 exactly. The reconcile body contains NO `.await`, NO `Instant::now()` / `SystemTime::now()`, NO direct IntentStore/ObservationStore/ViewStore writes, NO DB handle (verified by dst-lint gate on PR; the `reconcile` fn is pure-sync per ADR-0035). `fingerprint(&ServiceVip, &[Backend]) -> BackendSetFingerprint` pure fn exists; deterministic across runs (proptest: same inputs → same output across N invocations). Pure-Rust unit tests in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/backend_discovery_bridge/mod.rs` cover: (a) empty `desired.listeners` → zero actions emitted (S-BDB-09 unit-level proxy); (b) single listener + single Running alloc + empty view → one `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow` emitted with `backends.len() == 1` and `next_view.last_written_fingerprint[service_id]` populated; (c) dedup branch — second call with same inputs + view-from-prior-call emits zero actions (S-BDB-05 unit-level proxy); (d) GC branch — removing a service from `desired.listeners` shrinks `next_view.last_written_fingerprint` (S-BDB-07 unit-level proxy); (e) multi-replica — N Running allocs produce `backends.len() == N` (S-BDB-04 unit-level proxy); (f) terminated alloc — Running → empty Running set on next tick removes that backend from emitted row (S-BDB-03 unit-level proxy). Workspace `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean; clippy -D warnings clean.
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-02, S-BDB-03, S-BDB-04, S-BDB-05, S-BDB-07, S-BDB-09 (all unit-level proxies) | Unit tests: reconcile_empty_listeners_emits_zero_actions, reconcile_single_alloc_emits_one_action, reconcile_dedup_branch_emits_zero_actions_on_unchanged_inputs, reconcile_gc_branch_drops_removed_service_id, reconcile_multi_replica_emits_all_backends, reconcile_terminated_alloc_drops_backend, fingerprint_deterministic_across_runs
steps.implementation_notes: Unit tests live inline under `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` in the reconciler's mod.rs — these are pure-function-level tests (no DST harness, no SimObservationStore wiring) per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Adding a new test — which tier?". The DST invariants in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/backend_discovery_bridge.rs` close in 01-05 once hydration (01-03) and action-shim (01-04) are wired — the DST harness needs the full registration surface to drive the reconciler under fault interleavings. The `BackendDiscoveryBridge::new(host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr, writer_node_id: NodeId)` constructor takes both as MANDATORY parameters (per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Port-trait dependencies" — required, not defaulted at the call site; do NOT default `writer_node_id` in the constructor body). The reconciler is registered with a unique `ReconcilerName` identifier (the slug `"backend-discovery-bridge"`); the broker enqueues and routes evaluations to the bridge's `reconcile` method by name — observable behavior: an enqueue against the bridge's name reaches this reconciler's `reconcile` body and no other reconciler's.

steps.name: Runtime hydration arms (hydrate_desired + hydrate_actual) for BackendDiscoveryBridge
steps.description: Wire the bridge into the reconciler runtime's I/O boundary per architecture.md § 4.5. Per ADR-0036 the runtime owns hydration end-to-end — the reconciler is pure over pre-computed inputs. (1) `hydrate_desired` new match arm: read intent at `IntentKey::for_workload(&workload_id)` via `WorkloadIntent::from_store_bytes`, match on `WorkloadIntent::Service(ServiceV1)`, compute `spec_digest = intent.spec_digest()?`, consult `state.allocator.lock().await.get(&spec_digest)` for `assigned_vip` (per ADR-0049 § 5a — the bridge's `state.allocator` is the same `Arc<tokio::sync::Mutex<PersistentServiceVipAllocator>>` field on AppState introduced by the service-vip-allocator feature; Phase 1 invariant: memo populated synchronously at admission per ADR-0049 § 4, so this is always `Some(_)` for a persisted Service intent), project listeners `service_v1.listeners.iter().map(|l| (ServiceId::derive(&assigned_vip, l.port, "service-map"), ProjectedListener { vip: assigned_vip.clone(), port: l.port, protocol: l.protocol }))`. `WorkloadIntent::Job(_) | WorkloadIntent::Schedule(_)` → empty `BTreeMap` (only Service produces listener rows per ADR-0050 § 2). On allocator-memo absent (`None`) — log structured `tracing::debug!(name: "bridge.allocator_memo_absent", ...)` event and return empty `desired` (deferred convergence; § 18 retries). (2) `hydrate_actual` new match arm: `state.obs.alloc_status_rows_for_workload(&workload_id).await` filter to `state == AllocState::Running`, collect into `BTreeSet<AllocationId>`. (3) The `.await` on `state.allocator.lock()` lives in the hydrate path (runtime's I/O boundary), NOT in reconcile (which stays pure-sync per ADR-0035). (4) Update the runtime's `register` path to handle the new `AnyReconciler::BackendDiscoveryBridge` variant — `bulk_load::<BackendDiscoveryBridgeView>` and materialize the `BTreeMap<TargetResource, View>` per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Reconciler I/O" → "Boot / register-time". NO production-boot registration yet (lands 01-04 alongside action-shim); the runtime's `register` signature handles the variant but no actual `runtime.register(backend_discovery_bridge(...))` call exists in serve_with_config until 01-04. NO RED scaffolds closed in this step. Use `BTreeSet` / `BTreeMap` throughout (no `HashMap` / `HashSet`).
steps.criteria: `hydrate_desired` match arm for `AnyReconciler::BackendDiscoveryBridge(_)` exists at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs`; reads via `WorkloadIntent::from_store_bytes`; matches `Service(ServiceV1)`; computes `spec_digest`; consults `state.allocator.lock().await.get(&spec_digest)`; projects per-listener `(ServiceId, ProjectedListener)` entries; returns empty `BTreeMap` for `Job(_) | Schedule(_)`. `hydrate_actual` match arm exists; reads `alloc_status_rows_for_workload(&workload_id)`; filters to `Running`; collects `BTreeSet<AllocationId>`. Runtime `register` path handles the new `AnyReconciler::BackendDiscoveryBridge` variant; `bulk_load::<BackendDiscoveryBridgeView>` populates the in-memory `BTreeMap<TargetResource, View>` per ADR-0035 boot semantics. Unit tests in the runtime module cover: (a) hydrate_desired Service variant returns N listeners projected with allocator-issued VIP for N-listener Service spec (S-BDB-10 unit-level proxy); (b) hydrate_desired Job variant returns empty listeners (S-BDB-08 unit-level proxy); (c) hydrate_desired Schedule variant returns empty listeners (S-BDB-08 unit-level proxy); (d) hydrate_desired allocator-memo-absent returns empty listeners + emits structured tracing debug event named `bridge.allocator_memo_absent`; (e) hydrate_actual returns only Running allocs (Pending / Failed / Terminated filtered out). Workspace check / clippy clean. The dst-lint gate accepts the new arms (no `Instant::now` / `tokio::net::*` / `std::env::*` calls in `core` or `control-plane` reconciler code paths).
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-02 (success), S-BDB-08 (Job/Schedule skip), S-BDB-10 (multi-listener), S-BDB-16 (host_ipv4 plumbed at runtime boundary) | Unit tests: hydrate_desired_service_projects_listeners_with_allocator_vip, hydrate_desired_job_returns_empty_listeners, hydrate_desired_schedule_returns_empty_listeners, hydrate_desired_allocator_memo_absent_returns_empty_and_logs_debug, hydrate_actual_filters_to_running_only
steps.implementation_notes: `tokio::sync::Mutex` (not `parking_lot::Mutex`) per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Concurrency & async" — the lock spans an `.await`. The hydrate code acquires `state.allocator.lock().await`, performs the sync `get(&spec_digest)`, then drops the guard before any further `.await`. The structured debug event uses `tracing::debug!(name: "bridge.allocator_memo_absent", workload_id = %workload_id, spec_digest = ?spec_digest, "VIP allocator memo absent for Service intent; deferring tick")` (per architecture.md § 4.5). DO NOT preemptively wire this into the production boot registration list — that's deliberately deferred to 01-04 so the action-shim dispatch lands in the same commit and the broker's enqueue site sees a complete bridge surface in one atomic landing.

steps.name: Action shim write_service_backend_row + boot-time bridge registration
steps.description: Land the bridge's action-shim dispatch wrapper per architecture.md § 4.4 and register the bridge in `serve_with_config` per architecture.md § 4.7. (1) `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/write_service_backend_row.rs` `pub async fn dispatch(action: &Action, observation: &dyn ObservationStore) -> Result<(), ObservationStoreError>` — pattern-matches on `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow { row, correlation: _ }` and calls `observation.write(ObservationRow::ServiceBackend(row.clone())).await`. No correlation-driven follow-up at the shim (bridge's next tick observes its own write via the dedup fingerprint per architecture.md § 4.4). Panics on wrong variant (caller is the action-shim match arm — the sole expected caller; this is the established precedent across action-shim wrappers). (2) Wire the new dispatch arm into the action-shim's exhaustive `match action { ... }` block (the compile-time exhaustive-match enforcement per architecture.md § 8.4 / ADR-0023 forces this — without it, the match becomes non-exhaustive and `cargo check` rejects). (3) Add `pub fn backend_discovery_bridge(host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr, node_id: NodeId) -> AnyReconciler` constructor at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`. (4) In `serve_with_config`, after the existing `bulk_load_service_vip_allocator` block (lib.rs:667-679 in current HEAD per architecture.md § 4.7) and after the existing `workload_lifecycle()` registration call, add `runtime.register(backend_discovery_bridge(host_ipv4, node_id.clone())).await?;` BEFORE the existing `service_map_hydrator()` registration call — the bridge writes for the hydrator to consume. (5) The `host_ipv4` parameter to `backend_discovery_bridge(...)` is sourced from `AppState.host_ipv4` (lands in Phase 02, step 02-01). For Phase 01, MANDATE: introduce a `host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr = Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST` placeholder threaded into the bridge constructor in `serve_with_config` (single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md`); 02-01 replaces the placeholder with the `resolve_iface_ipv4(&dataplane_cfg.client_iface)?` output in a one-line edit. Deferring the `runtime.register(backend_discovery_bridge(...))` call to Phase 02 is FORBIDDEN — the bridge MUST be registered in Phase 01's production boot so the Phase 01 closing step's DST invariants (`BridgeEventuallyWritesBackendRow`, `BridgeIdempotentSteadyState`, `BridgeRecomputesFingerprintOnReplay` per 01-05) can fire: without registration the broker never enqueues the bridge, the reconciler runtime never ticks it, and the invariant assertions are unreachable. Per ADR-0052 § "Trigger / hydration shape", the bridge MUST be enqueued alongside `WorkloadLifecycle` on `AllocStatusRow` changes — that requires the bridge to be live in the registry from Phase 01 onward. (6) Update `exit_observer` (or the existing broker re-enqueue site for `WorkloadLifecycle`) to ALSO re-enqueue the `BackendDiscoveryBridge` reconciler when an `AllocStatusRow` transition is observed for a workload — per architecture.md § 3 step 5: "Broker re-enqueues `BackendDiscoveryBridge` for the workload (same enqueue site as `WorkloadLifecycle`, keyed by `WorkloadId`)."
steps.criteria: `action_shim::write_service_backend_row::dispatch` exists with the signature in architecture.md § 4.4. Action-shim exhaustive `match action { ... }` block updated; cargo check workspace clean (the exhaustiveness check is the load-bearing enforcement). `backend_discovery_bridge(host_ipv4, node_id)` constructor exists at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`. `serve_with_config` registers the bridge via `runtime.register(backend_discovery_bridge(...))` between the `workload_lifecycle()` and `service_map_hydrator()` registrations. `exit_observer` (or the broker re-enqueue site) re-enqueues `BackendDiscoveryBridge` for workload-scoped `AllocStatusRow` transitions, matching the existing `WorkloadLifecycle` enqueue shape. Pure-Rust unit tests cover: (a) dispatch with `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow` writes one `ObservationRow::ServiceBackend(row)` to a `SimObservationStore`; (b) dispatch panics with wrong-variant `Action::HttpCall { ... }` (or any non-matching variant) — tested via `#[should_panic(expected = "action_shim::write_service_backend_row::dispatch invoked with wrong Action variant")]`. Workspace check / clippy / nextest --no-run clean. RED scaffold gates still in place: invariant stubs in 01-05; walking_skeleton.rs + boot_composition.rs `#[should_panic]` still active.
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-02 (action-shim write path) | Unit tests: dispatch_writes_observation_row, dispatch_panics_on_wrong_variant
steps.implementation_notes: Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § Errors / pass-through, the dispatch wrapper does NOT re-wrap the typed `ObservationStoreError` — returns it directly via `?`-pass-through to the action-shim's outer dispatch loop. The `host_ipv4` placeholder in (5) is a single-commit transitional shape — 02-01 replaces it with the resolved value from `resolve_iface_ipv4(&dataplane_cfg.client_iface)?`. Per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md`, do NOT introduce a feature flag or a parallel boot path — the placeholder is internal-only inside `serve_with_config`'s body and survives at most one DELIVER commit. Verify the broker re-enqueue site by grepping `crates/overdrive-control-plane/` for `WorkloadLifecycle` and `enqueue_eval` (or equivalent) — the bridge enqueue lands next to that call.

steps.name: Close DST RED scaffolds: BridgeEventuallyWritesBackendRow + BridgeIdempotentSteadyState + BridgeRecomputesFingerprintOnReplay + Slice-1 quality gate
steps.description: Replace `todo!("RED scaffold: ...")` bodies in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/backend_discovery_bridge.rs` with real `impl Invariant for ...::evaluate` bodies per S-BDB-02..S-BDB-10 + S-BDB-06 (Atlas Q2). (1) `BridgeEventuallyWritesBackendRow` — for every Service workload with ≥ 1 listener AND allocator-issued VIP for its `spec_digest` AND ≥ 1 Running alloc, the harness's `SimObservationStore` eventually reports `service_backends_rows(service_id).backends == expected` (one Backend per Running alloc; `vip == assigned_vip`; `updated_at.counter == tick.tick + 1`; `updated_at.writer == AppState.node_id`). Eventual property — covers S-BDB-02/03/04/10. (2) `BridgeIdempotentSteadyState` — once steady state reached, K subsequent ticks (K ≥ 1) with unchanged inputs produce zero `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow` actions AND View's `last_written_fingerprint` unchanged AND no new obs row write. Also exercises the View GC retain clause via S-BDB-07: shrinking the listener set shrinks the View. Always property — covers S-BDB-05/07. (3) `BridgeRecomputesFingerprintOnReplay` (Atlas Q2) — harness injects crash between `SimViewStore::write_through` fsync and the runtime's in-memory `BTreeMap::insert` step, restarts, runs `bulk_load`, ticks; bridge's first post-restart tick re-projects from fresh inputs, recomputes per-service fingerprint, emits zero actions if fingerprint matches (idempotent) OR emits `Action::WriteServiceBackendRow` if differs (no silent skip). Eventually steady state reached. (4) Register the three invariants in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs`'s `Invariant` enum (already RED-scaffold-listed per DWD-09); each invariant variant calls into its struct's `evaluate(&self, harness: &Harness) -> Verdict`. (5) DST harness fault-injection: drive the seeded fault catalogue listed in `backend_discovery_bridge.rs` module docs — single Running alloc / multi alloc / Running→Failed→second Running / ViewStore fsync delay / SimObservationStore transient Busy / crash-between-fsync-and-insert. (6) Strip the `#![expect(clippy::todo, ...)]` crate-level attribute when the lint stops firing (per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Production-side scaffolds"). (7) Per-step mutation gate on the bridge files: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/backend_discovery_bridge/mod.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconcilers/backend_discovery_bridge/fingerprint.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/write_service_backend_row.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/worker/exit_observer.rs` — kill rate ≥ 80%. (8) Slice 1 closes GH #174 — the bridge is live in production boot under whichever Dataplane impl serve_with_config currently composes; #175's `EbpfDataplane` swap is Phase 02.
steps.criteria: `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/backend_discovery_bridge.rs` contains zero `todo!()` markers; `#![expect(clippy::todo, ...)]` attribute removed. All three invariants register in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs` `Invariant` enum and route to their `evaluate(&self, harness: &Harness) -> Verdict` bodies. `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo dst --workspace` (or equivalent — confirm xtask alias at PREPARE) passes with all three invariants asserting GREEN under the seeded fault catalogue documented in the file's module docs. The S-BDB-02/03/04/05/06/07/08/09/10 spec rows from `distill/test-scenarios.md` are guarded by at least one of the three invariants per the Production-code → scenario mapping table in distill/test-scenarios.md. Per-step mutation gate: kill rate ≥ 80% on files-touched-by-this-slice (bridge reconciler, fingerprint, action-shim, runtime hydration arms, lib boot composition, exit_observer enqueue). `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `pass` verdict. Workspace `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` clean (the walking_skeleton.rs + boot_composition.rs `#[should_panic]` tests still PASS because their RED scaffolds remain — Slice 2 closes them).
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-02, S-BDB-05, S-BDB-06 (Atlas Q2 — DST invariants close all three RED scaffolds) | Unit tests: bridge_eventually_writes_backend_row, bridge_idempotent_steady_state, bridge_recomputes_fingerprint_on_replay
steps.implementation_notes: Per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Mutation testing", the per-step mutation invocation MUST include `--features integration-tests` AND prefix with `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS (mandatory — without Lima the `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]` test surface is unreachable and kill rate is meaningless). The fault catalogue is the load-bearing fixture; do NOT silently shrink it during implementation. Atlas Q2 (S-BDB-06) is the structural defense against fsync-then-memory ordering drift — the invariant simulates the worst-case crash window and asserts the bridge re-projects. The `SimViewStore::inject_fsync_failure` (or equivalent crash-injection method) lands here if not already present in the sim adapter surface; coordinate at PREPARE with the existing SimViewStore implementation. After this step lands, GH #174 is fully closed (DST proves correctness; bridge is live in production registry). The walking-skeleton remains RED-scaffolded — Phase 02 closes it via real EbpfDataplane wiring.
acceptance_scenarios: S-BDB-06|(Atlas Q2 — DST invariants close all three RED scaffolds)

steps.name: [dataplane] config section + DataplaneBootError + resolve_iface_ipv4 helper + AppState.host_ipv4
steps.description: Land the non-XDP boot infrastructure per architecture.md § 5.1, § 5.3. (1) Add `[dataplane]` TOML section to the config parser with required `client_iface: String` + `backend_iface: String`. Missing section produces `ControlPlaneError::Validation { message: "missing required [dataplane] section in overdrive.toml (client_iface + backend_iface)", field: Some("dataplane") }` mirroring the existing `[tls]` precedent per ADR-0010. The `[dataplane.vip_allocator]` subsection (from the service-vip-allocator feature) coexists at the same `[dataplane]` root. (2) Add `DataplaneBootError` typed enum at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/error.rs` per architecture.md § 5.3 with three variants: `Construct { client_iface, backend_iface, source: DataplaneError }`, `Probe { source: DataplaneError }`, `IfaceAddrResolution { iface, source: io::Error }`. Each variant's `#[error("...")]` template includes operator-facing remediation steps (per architecture.md § 5.3 verbatim — `ip link show <iface>`, `mount | grep bpffs`, `dmesg | tail`, `ip -4 addr show <iface>`, etc.). Add `ControlPlaneError::DataplaneBoot(#[from] DataplaneBootError)` variant; `to_response` arm returns `StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR` (mirrors `ViewStoreBoot` / `Cgroup` / `CgroupBootstrap` precedent). Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § Errors → "Never flatten a typed error to Internal(String)" — use `#[from]`, NOT `.map_err(|e| ControlPlaneError::internal(...))`. (3) `resolve_iface_ipv4(iface: &str) -> Result<Ipv4Addr, io::Error>` helper at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/iface.rs` (new module) using `getifaddrs` via the `nix` crate (or the existing FFI surface used elsewhere in the workspace — verify at PREPARE). Returns `Err(io::Error)` with `ErrorKind::NotFound` when iface has no IPv4 address; `ErrorKind::Other` for other `getifaddrs` failures. (4) `AppState.host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr` field added; `AppState::new` signature gains a mandatory `host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr` parameter (NOT defaulted per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Port-trait dependencies"). (5) Migrate every `AppState::new(...)` test fixture call site (~20 sites, mirrors the precedent cascade from 02-03c of service-vip-allocator) to pass `host_ipv4`; tests can pass `Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST` since they do not exercise the real-iface resolution path. (6) Replace the temporary `host_ipv4 = Ipv4Addr::LOCALHOST` placeholder in `serve_with_config` (introduced in 01-04) with the real `resolve_iface_ipv4(&dataplane_cfg.client_iface).map_err(|source| DataplaneBootError::IfaceAddrResolution { iface: dataplane_cfg.client_iface.clone(), source })?` call. (7) NO EbpfDataplane wiring yet (lands 02-02) — `serve_with_config` continues to compose whichever dataplane it composes today (NoopDataplane or the test-injected SimDataplane). (8) Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Distinct failure modes get distinct error variants" — DataplaneBootError variants are propagated via `?`-into-`#[from]`, never absorbed into a generic `Internal(String)`.
steps.criteria: Workspace `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean; clippy -D warnings clean. `[dataplane]` TOML section parsed with required `client_iface` + `backend_iface`. Missing section → `ControlPlaneError::Validation { ... field: Some("dataplane"), ... }` (S-BDB-12 close-out — see scenario file). `DataplaneBootError` enum exists at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/error.rs` with three variants matching architecture.md § 5.3. `ControlPlaneError::DataplaneBoot(#[from] DataplaneBootError)` variant exists; `to_response` arm returns `StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR`. `resolve_iface_ipv4(iface: &str) -> Result<Ipv4Addr, io::Error>` helper at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/iface.rs`. `AppState.host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr` field; `AppState::new` signature gains mandatory `host_ipv4` parameter; all ~20 fixture call sites migrated. `serve_with_config` calls `resolve_iface_ipv4(&dataplane_cfg.client_iface)` and routes failure through `DataplaneBootError::IfaceAddrResolution`. Unit tests cover: (a) `resolve_iface_ipv4` on `lo` (loopback) returns `127.0.0.1` (verify at PREPARE — may need feature gating for the Lima env); (b) `resolve_iface_ipv4` on `bogus-iface-foo` returns `Err` with `ErrorKind::NotFound`; (c) `DataplaneBootError::IfaceAddrResolution` `Display` form contains the iface name AND the substring `ip -4 addr show`. The walking_skeleton.rs + boot_composition.rs `#[should_panic]` scaffolds untouched in this step. RED scaffolds still active for S-BDB-11, S-BDB-13, S-BDB-14, S-BDB-15, S-BDB-16, S-BDB-17, S-BDB-18, S-BDB-20 (Phase 02 later steps); S-BDB-12 may close in this step since it's pure-config-error (no XDP needed).
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-12 (missing [dataplane] section), S-BDB-16 (host_ipv4 resolution success), S-BDB-17 (resolution failure) | Unit tests: boot_refuses_when_dataplane_section_missing, resolve_iface_ipv4_loopback, resolve_iface_ipv4_nonexistent_iface_returns_not_found, dataplane_boot_error_iface_addr_resolution_display_contains_remediation
steps.implementation_notes: S-BDB-12 (missing [dataplane] section) is the simplest boot-refusal scenario and may close in this step (no XDP / no Lima needed); replace its `#[should_panic]` body with the real assertion. Per DWD-10: S-BDB-12 enables alongside S-BDB-02 first in Slice 2. The remaining boot-composition RED scaffolds (S-BDB-11, S-BDB-13, S-BDB-14, S-BDB-15, S-BDB-16, S-BDB-17, S-BDB-18, S-BDB-20) close in 02-02 / 02-03 / 02-04 as their underlying production code lands. Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § Errors / Distinct failure modes — `resolve_iface_ipv4` MUST surface `ErrorKind::NotFound` (no IPv4) distinctly from `ErrorKind::Other` (getifaddrs syscall failure); do NOT absorb both into a generic error. The ~20-fixture cascade for `AppState::new(host_ipv4: Ipv4Addr, ...)` mirrors 02-03c of service-vip-allocator (precedent for the migration pattern). Use `nix::ifaddrs::getifaddrs()` if `nix` is already in workspace; otherwise use the `socket2`-based approach if that's the existing precedent for syscalls in this crate.

steps.name: Boot composition: EbpfDataplane::new + XDP attach + Drop RAII + attach-mode fallback emit
steps.description: Replace `NoopDataplane` with `EbpfDataplane::new(&client_iface, &backend_iface)` in `serve_with_config` per architecture.md § 5.2. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations.md` — delete `NoopDataplane` from production boot path; retained as a test fixture under `overdrive-host` only if grep finds active test users, deleted otherwise. (1) `serve_with_config` constructs `overdrive_dataplane::EbpfDataplane::new(&dataplane_cfg.client_iface, &dataplane_cfg.backend_iface)` and routes construction failure through `DataplaneBootError::Construct { client_iface, backend_iface, source }` per architecture.md § 5.3. (2) The constructed `EbpfDataplane` is wrapped in `Arc<dyn overdrive_core::traits::dataplane::Dataplane>` and passed through the action-shim wiring exactly as the current `NoopDataplane` wiring does. (3) The `EbpfDataplane::new` per-iface attach loop emits `tracing::warn!(name: "xdp.attach.fallback_generic", iface = %iface, errno = ?e, ...)` when native attach is rejected with `EOPNOTSUPP` / `ENOTSUP` and the loader retries with `XdpFlags::SKB_MODE` per architecture.md § 5.5. The `should_fallback_to_generic` classifier already exists in `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs` per the architecture.md cross-reference; emit the structured event AT the moment the fallback decision is taken, per-iface. (4) `impl Drop for EbpfDataplane` per architecture.md § 5.6 — unlinks the SERVICE_MAP bpffs pin at `self.pin_dir.join(SERVICE_MAP_NAME)`, `XdpLinkId` auto-detach via aya. Failure on `remove_file` logs `tracing::debug!(name: "xdp.shutdown.unlink_failed", ...)` for non-`NotFound` `ErrorKind` (panic propagation in-flight; cannot bubble errors). (5) The `LimaFixture::setup_veth_pair()` test fixture exists already per `crates/overdrive-dataplane/tests/integration/atomic_swap.rs` precedent (verify at PREPARE); the boot-composition tests reuse it. (6) Close RED `#[should_panic]` scaffolds for S-BDB-11 (happy-path boot attaches XDP to both ifaces), S-BDB-13 (invalid iface refuses boot), S-BDB-16 (resolve_iface_ipv4 happy path), S-BDB-17 (no-IPv4 iface refuses boot), S-BDB-18 (graceful shutdown via Drop), S-BDB-20 (attach-mode fallback emit). NO Earned-Trust probe yet (lands 02-03) — boot can succeed past `new()` and proceed to listener bind without a probe call. (7) Per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Trait definitions specify behavior" — `EbpfDataplane::new` constructor's docstring pins the observable behavior: returns `Ok(Self)` when XDP attached to both ifaces (native or generic), returns `Err(DataplaneError::IfaceNotFound { iface })` when `if_nametoindex` fails for either iface, returns `Err(DataplaneError::LoadFailed(_))` when XDP attach fails for both native AND generic on either iface.
steps.criteria: `serve_with_config` constructs `EbpfDataplane::new(&client_iface, &backend_iface)` and routes construction failure through `DataplaneBootError::Construct`. `NoopDataplane` is no longer composed in production boot (grep `serve_with_config` for `NoopDataplane` returns zero matches). `impl Drop for EbpfDataplane` exists and unlinks the SERVICE_MAP bpffs pin on drop. The `xdp.attach.fallback_generic` structured event fires per-iface when native attach is rejected with `EOPNOTSUPP` / `ENOTSUP`. Tier 3 Lima tests closed: S-BDB-11 (happy-path boot composes EbpfDataplane, XDP attached to both ifaces, SERVICE_MAP bpffs pin exists at `/sys/fs/bpf/overdrive/SERVICE_MAP` per `bpftool prog show` + `ip link show <iface>`); S-BDB-13 (invalid iface returns `ControlPlaneError::DataplaneBoot(DataplaneBootError::Construct { source: DataplaneError::IfaceNotFound { iface } })` with `Display` containing `ip link show <iface>` suggestion); S-BDB-16 (resolve_iface_ipv4 happy path returns configured iface's IPv4, AppState carries it); S-BDB-17 (no-IPv4 iface returns `DataplaneBootError::IfaceAddrResolution` with `Display` containing `ip -4 addr show <iface>`); S-BDB-18 (graceful shutdown: `ip link show lb_veth_a` and `ip link show lb_veth_b` show NO XDP attachment after Drop, SERVICE_MAP pin removed); S-BDB-20 (attach to `dummy0` emits exactly one structured `xdp.attach.fallback_generic` event with `iface = "dummy0"` AND `errno = EOPNOTSUPP`, SKB_MODE retry succeeds, `ip link show dummy0` shows `xdpgeneric`). RED scaffolds remain for S-BDB-01 (walking-skeleton e2e — closes 02-04), S-BDB-12 (closed in 02-01 already), S-BDB-14 / S-BDB-15 (Earned-Trust probe — closes 02-03), S-BDB-19 (in-process hydrator handoff — closes 02-04). Workspace `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -E 'test(boot_composition)' --features integration-tests` passes.
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-11 (boot composes EbpfDataplane + XDP attach), S-BDB-13 (non-existent iface), S-BDB-16 (host_ipv4 plumbed), S-BDB-17 (no IPv4 on iface), S-BDB-18 (Drop RAII), S-BDB-20 (attach-mode fallback) | Unit tests: boot_composes_ebpf_dataplane_and_attaches_xdp_to_both_ifaces, boot_refuses_when_client_iface_does_not_exist, boot_resolves_host_ipv4_via_getifaddrs_on_client_iface, boot_refuses_when_iface_has_no_ipv4_address, graceful_shutdown_detaches_xdp_and_removes_bpffs_pin, attach_mode_fallback_emits_structured_event_on_dummy_iface
steps.implementation_notes: Per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running tests — Lima VM" — every Tier 3 test runs through `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS. The Lima VM (`infra/lima/overdrive-dev.yaml`) provisions `lb_veth_a` / `lb_veth_b` per the existing `atomic_swap.rs` precedent; verify the setup helper is reusable from boot_composition.rs at PREPARE. S-BDB-18 (shutdown) MUST be tested via explicit `drop(server)` in the test body — the `Drop` impl is the production code under test. SIGKILL recovery is OUT OF SCOPE per scenario notes (operator-side discipline per `.claude/rules/debugging.md` § "Leftover XDP attachments"). Per `.claude/rules/debugging.md` § "Leftover XDP attachments across runs" — every Tier 3 boot test in this file MUST clean up leftover XDP attachments and bpffs pins in its teardown to avoid cross-test contamination; the existing dataplane integration tests have the cleanup precedent. S-BDB-12 (missing section) closed in 02-01; do NOT touch its test body here. Single-cut deletion: if grep finds NO active test users of `NoopDataplane` outside the production boot path, delete the entire `NoopDataplane` impl from `crates/overdrive-host/src/dataplane.rs` in this commit.

steps.name: EbpfDataplane::probe Earned-Trust + boot-time invocation + DataplaneBootError::Probe wiring
steps.description: Land the Earned-Trust probe per architecture.md § 5.4 and CLAUDE.md principle 12 ("Every dependency you don't probe is an act of faith you made for the user"). The probe proves the HoM pin-by-name reuse path + BACKEND_MAP programmability work on THIS kernel BEFORE accepting traffic. (1) `EbpfDataplane::probe(&self) -> Result<(), DataplaneError>` body at `crates/overdrive-dataplane/src/lib.rs` per architecture.md § 5.4 verbatim: insert sentinel `BackendId::PROBE = u32::MAX` with `BackendEntryPod { ipv4: 0x7F_00_00_01, port: 0, weight: 0, healthy: 0, _pad: 0 }` via the typed `BackendMapHandle::insert`; read back via `BackendMapHandle::get`; assert byte-equal; delete via `BackendMapHandle::remove`. Each step's failure routes to `DataplaneError::LoadFailed(format!("probe: ... : {e}"))`. The probe deletes the sentinel on success so `BACKEND_MAP::get(u32::MAX, 0)` returns `None` after probe. (2) `serve_with_config` calls `ebpf_dataplane.probe().await.map_err(|source| DataplaneBootError::Probe { source })?` AFTER `EbpfDataplane::new(...)` succeeds and BEFORE any other dataplane operation (composition-root invariant per architecture.md § 5.4 + CLAUDE.md principle 12c — "wire then probe then use"). (3) Failure surfaces structured `tracing::warn!(name: "health.startup.refused", reason = "dataplane.probe", ...)` event per architecture.md § 5.4 and CLAUDE.md principle 12b. (4) Close RED `#[should_panic]` scaffolds for S-BDB-14 (probe failure refuses boot — verify the failure-injection seam at PREPARE; recommended shape per scenario file is `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "integration-tests"))]` field `probe_fault: Option<DataplaneError>` on `EbpfDataplane` that `probe()` returns preferentially when set; alternatively DELIVER may inject via real BACKEND_MAP corruption) and S-BDB-15 (probe success advances boot — assert `BACKEND_MAP::get(u32::MAX, 0)` returns `None` after probe; boot reaches listener bind). (5) Per CLAUDE.md principle 12c ("Earned Trust enforced via three semantically orthogonal layers") — the probe contract is documented in the `Dataplane` trait's `probe` method rustdoc per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Trait definitions specify behavior": preconditions (none), postconditions (BACKEND_MAP is byte-equal to its pre-probe state on `Ok(())`; on `Err`, the BACKEND_MAP MAY contain partial sentinel state and the dataplane MUST NOT be used). NOTE: the existing dst-lint subtype check + AST scanner from `xtask/src/dst_lint.rs` already cover layers 1 and 2 of CLAUDE.md principle 12c for the broader port-trait surface; this step does NOT extend dst-lint — the probe is a single method on a single concrete type, not a cross-cutting port-trait constraint.
steps.criteria: `EbpfDataplane::probe(&self) -> Result<(), DataplaneError>` body matches architecture.md § 5.4 verbatim. `serve_with_config` calls `ebpf_dataplane.probe().await` AFTER `new()` succeeds and BEFORE composition. Probe failure routes to `ControlPlaneError::DataplaneBoot(DataplaneBootError::Probe { source: DataplaneError::LoadFailed(_) })` with `Display` form containing substring `probe: round-trip mismatch` OR `probe: BACKEND_MAP`. Structured `health.startup.refused` event fires on probe failure with `reason = "dataplane.probe"`. Tier 3 Lima tests closed: S-BDB-14 (probe failure refuses boot — `EbpfDataplane::new` succeeds, probe returns `Err(LoadFailed(_))` via injection seam, `serve_with_config` returns `Err`, `health.startup.refused` event observable, test fixture cleans up partial state); S-BDB-15 (probe success advances boot — `EbpfDataplane::new` succeeds, `probe` returns `Ok(())`, `BACKEND_MAP::get(u32::MAX, 0)` returns `None` after probe, boot reaches listener bind observable via successful HTTPS handshake). Workspace `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -E 'test(boot_composition)' --features integration-tests` passes (S-BDB-11, S-BDB-12, S-BDB-13, S-BDB-14, S-BDB-15, S-BDB-16, S-BDB-17, S-BDB-18, S-BDB-20 all GREEN). RED scaffolds remain for S-BDB-01 (walking-skeleton — closes 02-04) and S-BDB-19 (in-process hydrator handoff — closes 02-04).
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-14 (Earned-Trust probe failure refuses boot), S-BDB-15 (probe success advances boot) | Unit tests: boot_refuses_when_earned_trust_probe_fails, boot_succeeds_when_earned_trust_probe_round_trips_backend_map
steps.implementation_notes: Per CLAUDE.md principle 12 ("Earned Trust — CRITICAL"): the probe contract is the structural defense against "the world is honest" assumptions. The probe MUST exercise the SPECIFIC kernel surface that production traffic will hit — for HoM-pin-by-name reuse, that's the BACKEND_MAP write-read-delete round-trip through the typed `BackendMapHandle`. DO NOT shortcut to a `Result::Ok(())` stub for the probe body — the probe IS the proof. The failure-injection seam at S-BDB-14: per scenario file, the simplest shape is a `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "integration-tests"))]` field `probe_fault: Option<DataplaneError>` on `EbpfDataplane`; the probe body checks `if let Some(fault) = &self.probe_fault { return Err(fault.clone()); }` BEFORE the real probe steps. DELIVER may instead drive failure via a real BACKEND_MAP corruption (insert a non-sentinel value at `u32::MAX` before boot) if the test seam adds too much production-type surface. The composition-root invariant "wire then probe then use" (CLAUDE.md principle 12c) is enforced at the `serve_with_config` call site — the probe call MUST sit between `new()` and the first action-shim dispatch through the dataplane.

steps.name: Walking-skeleton (S-BDB-01) Tier 3 e2e + S-BDB-19 in-process hydrator handoff + per-PR mutation gate
steps.description: Land the joint #174+#175 walking-skeleton acceptance test per architecture.md § 6 + distill/walking-skeleton.md. This is the demo-able e2e gate per the user-centric framing: "I submit a Service workload with a TCP listener; I get back an allocator-issued VIP; I can open a TCP connection to that VIP and talk to my workload." (1) `TestServer` fixture at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/backend_discovery_bridge/test_server.rs` per Atlas Q1 disposition + DWD-09: lives in test directory, `pub fn serve_with_dataplane_config(DataplaneConfig { client_iface, backend_iface }) -> async`, binds OS-assigned port (`127.0.0.1:0`), exposes `submit_workload(spec) -> StreamingSubmitResponse` via real HTTPS client (NOT direct internal call — Mandate 1 / hexagonal boundary per DWD-07), exposes `read_workload_intent(name)` and `app_state()` as `pub(crate)` accessors. (2) `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "integration-tests"))]`-gated `EbpfDataplane::dataplane_inspect() -> BackendMapInspector / ServiceMapInspector` accessor exposing typed `BackendMapHandle::keys() / get()` + `HashOfMapsHandle::get_inner_entries()` per architecture.md § 6.2. Verify gating: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-dataplane` (no `integration-tests` feature) must NOT see `dataplane_inspect()` in the public API. (3) Echo listener exec command per DWD-03 K2 Form A — Python one-liner in the Service spec's `exec.cmd` field (Python 3 IS provisioned in Lima per verified package list 2026-05-20). If Form A is unworkable mid-implementation, fall back to Form B (Rust echo binary via `include_bytes!`) — if NEITHER form workable, surface to user as blocker BEFORE adding `socat` to Lima provisioning (per DWD-03 K2 fallback rule). (4) `poll_until(budget, cadence, async || Option<T>) -> Option<T>` test helper — 50ms cadence, 2s budget; may reuse `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/workload_lifecycle/wait.rs` precedent shape. (5) Walking-skeleton test body per architecture.md § 6.2 verbatim: submit Service spec with `id="walking-skeleton-svc"`, `replicas=1`, listener `(port=8080, protocol="tcp")` (NO `vip` field — `deny_unknown_fields` rejects per ADR-0051), `exec.cmd` = Form A echo loop; assert submit response carries `assigned_vip`; verify allocator memo populated (precondition sanity per `.claude/rules/debugging.md` § 7 altitude); wait for Running within 10s; poll BACKEND_MAP for entry matching `(host_ipv4, 8080)` within 2s; assert SERVICE_MAP resolves `ServiceKey { vip = assigned_vip (host-order), port = 8080, proto = 6 (TCP) }` to a non-empty inner map; D3 in-gate TCP round-trip — `poll_until(2s, 50ms, async || tcp_connect_write_read(assigned_vip, 8080, b"walking-skeleton-probe\n"))` returns `Some(response)` and `response == b"walking-skeleton-probe\n"`; cleanup via Drop on server + lima_fixture. (6) Close RED `#[should_panic]` scaffold for S-BDB-01 (walking-skeleton e2e) AND S-BDB-19 (in-process hydrator handoff — `ServiceMapHydrator` picks up bridge-written row and emits `DataplaneUpdateService`). S-BDB-19 is exercised by the walking-skeleton transitively at Tier 3; the explicit Tier 1 DST coverage is via the existing `service_map_hydrator` invariants extended to drive against bridge-written rows from S-BDB-02. (7) Per-PR mutation gate per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Mutation testing" → "Per-step vs per-PR scoping" — final per-PR run is `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-control-plane` AND `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-dataplane`. Kill rate ≥ 80% per project gate per CLAUDE.md § "Mutation Testing Strategy". (8) Quality gate per CLAUDE.md: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` clean; `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` clean (macOS-side compile gate); `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests -- -D warnings` clean; `cargo test --doc --workspace` clean; OpenAPI gate clean (if regen required); `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask dst` clean (Tier 1 invariants pass under the seeded fault catalogue).
steps.criteria: S-BDB-01 walking-skeleton GREEN at Tier 3 — submit Service spec → assigned_vip echoed in submit response → AllocState::Running reached within 10s → BACKEND_MAP contains entry matching `(host_ipv4, 8080)` within 2s → SERVICE_MAP resolves `ServiceKey { vip = assigned_vip, port = 8080, proto = 6 }` to non-empty inner map → TCP round-trip to `<assigned_vip>:8080` echoes `walking-skeleton-probe\n` byte-equal within 2s (K1 bind-readiness budget) → teardown succeeds via Drop (SERVICE_MAP bpffs pin removed). S-BDB-19 GREEN at Tier 1 DST — `ServiceMapHydrator` reads bridge-written `service_backends_rows` and emits `Action::DataplaneUpdateService { vip == bridge_written_row.vip, backends == bridge_written_row.backends }`; action shim dispatches to `SimDataplane::update_service`; `service_hydration_results` row with `status: Completed { fingerprint, ... }` observable. `TestServer` fixture lives entirely under `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/backend_discovery_bridge/test_server.rs` (NOT under `src/`); Atlas Q1 isolation verified: `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check -p overdrive-dataplane` (no `integration-tests` feature) compiles cleanly without `dataplane_inspect()` in public API. Per-PR mutation gate kill rate ≥ 80% on both `overdrive-control-plane` and `overdrive-dataplane`; `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` records `pass`. Full workspace quality gate per CLAUDE.md: nextest --workspace clean, nextest --no-run clean (macOS compile), clippy -D warnings clean, doctests clean, DST (`cargo xtask dst`) clean. GH #174 + GH #175 FULLY closed: every AC (#174-1, #174-2, #174-3, #174-4, #174-5, #175-1, #175-2, #175-3, #175-4, #175-5) has at least one passing scenario per the Production code → scenario mapping table in distill/test-scenarios.md. The demo per walking-skeleton.md § "Demo script" is executable end-to-end: `overdrive workload submit --kind service --listener tcp/8080 --exec '<echo loop>'` returns VIP; `echo "walking-skeleton-probe" | nc <VIP> 8080` echoes back.
steps.scenario_name: Acceptance: S-BDB-01 (walking-skeleton e2e TCP round-trip), S-BDB-19 (hydrator picks up bridge-written row) | Unit tests: submit_service_workload_tcp_round_trip_through_vip_succeeds, service_map_hydrator_picks_up_bridge_written_row_and_emits_dataplane_update_service
steps.implementation_notes: Per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running tests — Lima VM" — the walking-skeleton runs through `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane -E 'test(walking_skeleton)' --features integration-tests`. K1 bind-readiness wait per DWD-03 — 50ms cadence × 2s budget = 40 attempts; first successful `connect + write_all + read_exact` round-trip wins. K3 payload per DWD-03 — literal bytes `walking-skeleton-probe\n` (24 bytes including trailing newline; the newline is intentional — line-buffered Python echo loops need it to flush). Per Mandate 1 / DWD-07 CM-A — `TestServer::submit_workload` MUST issue a real HTTP request to the bound socket (reqwest or equivalent), NOT a direct call into application services. A scaffold that constructs `WorkloadIntent::Service` directly and hands it to the bridge bypasses the driving adapter and is a review rejection. Per `.claude/rules/debugging.md` § "Leftover XDP attachments across runs" — the walking-skeleton's teardown MUST clean up bpffs pins and XDP attachments; the existing dataplane integration tests have the cleanup precedent. The `assigned_vip` MUST be read from the submit echo (NOT hardcoded) since the allocator owns the address space. S-BDB-19 is exercised transitively via the Tier 3 walking-skeleton (the production hydrator IS exercised end-to-end) AND directly via Tier 1 DST against `SimObservationStore` + `SimDataplane` (the existing `service_map_hydrator` invariants extend in this step to drive against bridge-written rows). The full per-PR mutation gate is the gate CI runs; verify locally before push per CLAUDE.md commit hygiene.

validation.notes: Initial review flagged scenario_name field opacity (S-BDB-NN acceptance IDs were only in description/criteria text). Fixed by prepending 'Acceptance: S-BDB-XX, ...' to every scenario_name. All 20 S-BDB scenarios mapped to steps.

blockers_resolved.summary: AC coupling — grep-verification instruction removed; replaced with behavioral outcome on reconciler registrability/routability.
Deferral ambiguity — option (b) deferring bridge registration to next phase removed; mandate placeholder host_ipv4 + single-cut replace in next phase's first step so DST invariants can fire.

passing_checks: External validity — user-facing invocation via HTTPS handler
Scenario coverage — all 20 S-BDB-NN scenarios mapped to steps
DST invariant closure — 3 invariants scheduled in final Phase 01 step
Architecture fidelity — ADR-0052, ADR-0035/0036, Earned-Trust composition-root
Sequencing — acyclic dependencies; Phase 02 gated on Phase 01 close
Unit-test boundary — hexagonal driving-port enforcement throughout
Project rules — single-cut migrations, BTreeMap-only, Lima prefixing, per-step + per-PR mutation gates, RED scaffold closure
non_blocking_notes: Conditional closure of the missing-config-section scenario in the first Phase 02 step is acceptable (pure config error; no XDP / Lima needed).
Walking-skeleton echo form fallback (Python → Rust → socat) appropriately defers tool choice to DELIVER crafter.
Length-limit warnings from `des.cli.roadmap validate` are cosmetic per project CLAUDE.md (scenario-level specificity is load-bearing).
